settings.text: Most neck guards use basic foam or low-grade materials that tear under pressure. Titan is built with Titanotex™, a proprietary fabric rated A9 for cut resistance, the highest standard in hockey.

When it comes to preventing lethal neck injuries, cut resistance alone isn’t enough – and neither is coverage on its own. Real protection demands both. A high ANSI rating means nothing if the collar leaves the carotid artery exposed. And wide coverage is worthless if the fabric can’t stop a skate blade. That’s why we don't compromise. We engineered Titanotex™ with up to A9 cut resistance, then paired it with our patent-pending Arc~Guard™ collar, designed to shield the most critical areas with zero gaps, zero shift, and zero excuses. Cut Resistance + Coverage = Real Safety. It’s not a nice-to-have.
It’s the only equation that works.

settings.heading: WHY CUT RESISTANCE LEVELS MATTER MORE THAN BRAND NAMES

settings.text:
In a Mayo Clinic study simulating real hockey accidents, nearly every neck guard failed when hit with the kind of force a sharp skate creates at game speed. Only guards made with high-grade materials – equivalent to ANSI A6 or higher – were strong enough to stop the blade.

That’s why A6 isn’t a nice-to-have. It’s the minimum level of protection your player needs.

We’re not going to flood you with cherry-picked quotes. Instead, we built an entire page of unfiltered reviews, where you can sort by topic and see what real players (not paid actors) actually think. Video receipts included.
